headline: SK Hynix prices record-breaking Nasdaq ADR at $149; $26.5B raise is largest foreign-issuer listing ever
summary: SK Hynix confirmed its Nasdaq ADR issue price at $149/share overnight, raising $26.51 billion — the largest ever share sale by a non-U.S. company. The offering was 7x oversubscribed. Temporary ticker SKHYV trades from today, with regular SKHY from Jul 13. Proceeds fund Korean fab expansion and ASML EUV scanner procurement. Establishes a new listed HBM pure-play and resets peer valuation frames for MU and Samsung.

headline: Meta to mass-produce in-house 'Iris' AI ASIC from September; targets 14GW compute by 2027
summary: Internal Meta memos reviewed by Reuters detail the September mass-production start of 'Iris,' Meta's Broadcom-partnered TSMC-fabricated recommendation-ranking ASIC. Meta plans to double datacenter capacity from 7 GW in 2026 to 14 GW in 2027, with $145B AI infra spend this year and a new custom AI processor cadence every six months through 2027. Reduces Nvidia/AMD dependence but does not replace GPUs. META shares -2% on capex reset; AVGO benefits directly as Iris design partner.

headline: TSMC June monthly revenue release postponed to July 13 after Taiwan typhoon day-off
summary: TSMC delayed its scheduled Jul 10 June-revenue disclosure by one trading day after a typhoon-related day-off closed Taipei markets. Report now expected Jul 13. Investors watching for a third consecutive >25% YoY monthly print as a supply-side sustain check into the Jul 16 earnings call. Citi and UBS have TSMC price targets of NT$3,800 and NT$3,400 respectively into the call.

headline: Chip cohort extends pre-market rebound Friday: MU +4.5%, SNDK +7.6%, SMH +2.5%
summary: Semiconductor stocks led pre-market gains Friday morning as the SK Hynix ADR debut catalyzed HBM peer re-rating and the mid-week China H200 approval for Alibaba/ByteDance/DeepSeek (<200k unit cap) continued to unwind China-overhang shorts. Micron and SanDisk paced the storage cohort ahead of MU fiscal Q4. Intel lagged on Q2 earnings positioning (Jul 23). S&P 500 futures -0.22% into the open.

headline: Aalo-X experimental SMR targets criticality this month for AI datacenter co-location near INL
summary: Aalo Atomics' experimental Aalo-X small modular reactor is targeting first criticality in July 2026 as part of a co-location plan with an AI datacenter near Idaho National Laboratory. If achieved, it would be one of the earliest operational SMRs with a direct hyperscaler-adjacent load model, providing a real-world reference case for OKLO, SMR, XE and CEG buildout theses.

headline: Siemens Energy raises global new-power-plant demand outlook to 110-120 GW/yr from prior 100 GW ceiling
summary: Siemens Energy revised its annual global demand forecast for new power capacity to 110-120 GW/year, up from a prior ceiling of 100 GW. AI is now forecast to account for ~40% of total electricity consumption by 2030, up from ~20% today. The company is scaling U.S. production of transformers, switchgear and gas-turbine components, with Mississippi hosting its largest global grid-equipment factory (2028 completion). Direct read-across for GEV, ETN, VRT, and SNEXF backlogs.

headline: Starbucks building in-house AI tools to replace Microsoft inventory + IBM maintenance software
summary: Starbucks disclosed plans to develop AI-powered in-house alternatives to a Microsoft inventory-tracking system and an IBM maintenance-management tool, adding to the emerging 'AI-disruption of enterprise SaaS' narrative that pressured software stocks broadly on Thursday. Individually a small revenue line, but a template read for how large enterprises may reroute software budgets toward internal AI stacks, incrementally raising the customer-churn tail for MSFT, NOW, CRM and PLTR.

headline: Broadcom double-punch: Meta Iris ASIC partner + Apple $30B multi-year chip deal firms AVGO custom-silicon franchise
summary: Broadcom is riding two structural tailwinds this week: (1) named as Meta's design partner for the Iris AI ASIC entering mass production in September, and (2) a $30B+ multi-year cooperation agreement with Apple through 2031 for domestic manufacturing of 15B+ chips. The pairing entrenches AVGO's custom-silicon franchise across hyperscaler AI ASICs and premium consumer, arguably the most durable non-Nvidia AI-chip franchise in the market.
